title: cerastium latifolium proles uniflorum
intro: Cerastium latifolium proles uniflorum, also known as a variety of the broad-leaved mouse-ear chickweed, is a plant species within the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of Cerastium latifolium proles uniflorum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable remedy for skin conditions and minor infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in salves, ointments, and teas to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
